layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: TWO-COLUMN MARVIN TABLE LAYOUT with full-width title at top and full-width footer text at bottom. The slide uses a classic Marvin table structure (Section 4, Option A) to organize the two service offerings side by side as equal-weight columns. The title spans the full slide width at the top with a visible gap below it. The two content columns (Technology Platform and Capacity Building) are treated as equal-peer columns in a Marvin table, each containing a column header with underline, an icon/badge ornament, a subtitle, and a bullet list of capabilities. Columns are separated by a small gutter for visual breathing room. Dashed grey separator lines between bullet items create visual rhythm without consuming vertical space. The footer text sits at the bottom as a full-width text block with a visible top margin. The layout follows a top-to-bottom reading flow: title → table columns → footer. All elements snap to a 4-column grid with consistent margins and gutters. The design applies brand colors by role name only, uses dashed separators extensively per Section 0.10, and ensures content-aware spacing per Section 0.1.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: left-to-right
  Regions: 4
  Allocation: [0.12, 0.4, 0.4, 0.08]

Containers:
  C0 (LEFT COLUMN — Technology Platform): This container is the left column of a two-column Marvin table (Section 4, Option A). It occupies the left half of the slide body area, with equal width to the right column (split 1:1). The column structure from top to bottom: (1) Icon ornament — a small circular badge with a laptop/computer icon in brand accent color, positioned at the top-left of the column, sized proportionally to the header text height. (2) Column header — 'TECHNOLOGY PLATFORM' in bold, brand primary color, left-aligned, full column width. (3) Header underline — a solid horizontal line in brand dark color, full column width, placed directly beneath the header text. (4) Subtitle — 'Cryptocurrency Analytics Capabilities' in italic body font, brand text color, left-aligned, with a small top margin below the underline. (5) Bullet list — six bullet items describing platform capabilities. Each bullet uses a small brand-color circle as the bullet marker (not native PowerPoint bullets). Bullet text is body font, brand text color, left-aligned with a small left inset. Bullets are distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space using flexGrow on each bullet row section. (6) Dashed grey separator lines — thin horizontal dashed lines in light grey, full column width, placed between each consecutive bullet item (not after the last bullet). The separators create visual rhythm and enforce vertical alignment with the right column. The column has a small internal padding on all sides. No background fill, no border on the column itself. Content must fill the available vertical space without leaving a large gap at the bottom.
  C1 (RIGHT COLUMN — Capacity Building): This container is the right column of a two-column Marvin table (Section 4, Option A). It occupies the right half of the slide body area, with equal width to the left column (split 1:1). The column structure from top to bottom: (1) Icon ornament — a small circular badge with a graduation cap icon in brand accent color, positioned at the top-left of the column, sized proportionally to the header text height, matching the size and style of the left column icon. (2) Column header — 'CAPACITY BUILDING' in bold, brand primary color, left-aligned, full column width. (3) Header underline — a solid horizontal line in brand dark color, full column width, placed directly beneath the header text. (4) Subtitle — 'Training & Knowledge Transfer' in italic body font, brand text color, left-aligned, with a small top margin below the underline. (5) Bullet list — six bullet items describing training offerings. Each bullet uses a small brand-color circle as the bullet marker (not native PowerPoint bullets). Bullet text is body font, brand text color, left-aligned with a small left inset. Bullets are distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space using flexGrow on each bullet row section, with flexGrow values IDENTICAL to the left column so that row boundaries align horizontally across both columns. (6) Dashed grey separator lines — thin horizontal dashed lines in light grey, full column width, placed between each consecutive bullet item (not after the last bullet). The separators must be positioned at EXACTLY the same vertical positions as the separators in the left column to enforce horizontal alignment (Rule B2). The column has a small internal padding on all sides. No background fill, no border on the column itself. Content must fill the available vertical space without leaving a large gap at the bottom.
  C2 (FOOTER TEXT — Partnership Statement): This container holds the footer text that sits at the bottom of the slide, below the two-column table. It spans the full slide width (same left and right margins as the title). The text reads: 'Log-term partnerships including support training and ability to adapt and provide solutions to emerging needs' (note: the source text has a typo 'Log-term' which should be preserved as-is unless content correction is explicitly requested). The text is rendered in body font, brand text color, left-aligned, with a visible top margin separating it from the bottom edge of the table columns above. No background fill, no border, no decorative elements. The text sits on a single line or wraps naturally to 2–3 lines depending on font size, with a small bottom margin from the slide edge. This is a plain text block, not a colored footer bar.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER — Inactive): The bottom decorative layer is NOT ACTIVE for this slide. The two-column Marvin table structure fills the slide body area effectively, and the content density is sufficient that no additional background grouping element is needed. The columns are visually unified by their shared structure (matching headers, underlines, bullet styles, and aligned separator lines) without requiring a background card or panel. Per Rule D1, the decorative layer is used only when content would leave the slide looking empty or when visual grouping cannot be achieved through container design alone — neither condition applies here.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER — Inactive): The top connector layer is NOT ACTIVE for this slide. There are no cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements required. The two columns are independent content sections with no visual links between them. Per Rule E3, the top layer is used only for elements that genuinely span containers or sit above all containers — no such elements exist in this layout.
